text: Incident #482: api-gateway memory grew steadily for 40 minutes after a deploy due to an unbounded in-memory cache that never evicted old entries. Resolved by restarting the cache layer and adding a max-size eviction policy.

Incident #311: worker-queue backlog grew unbounded after a downstream dependency slowed down, causing memory usage to climb as messages piled up faster than they were processed. Resolved by adding backpressure and a queue depth alert.

Incident #267: a batch job processing large files caused a temporary CPU and disk I/O spike. Not a true anomaly, just an expected load pattern, but it was mistakenly flagged. Resolved by adding a maintenance-window exception.
Runbook: when api-gateway memory usage climbs steadily without a matching increase in request rate, check for cache growth first, then check for connection leaks in the upstream client pool.
Runbook: for sustained CPU saturation above 85 percent, check for a recent deploy, a runaway background job, or a missing rate limit before scaling out.
